evals.description: Adding prefill pages to 995 form with existing prefillTransformer

failed_assertions: Preserves the existing prefillTransformer (does not replace it wholesale)
Preserves the existing prefillTransformer (does not replace it wholesale)

evals.description: Full FE+BE implementation on burials-ez form

observations: Both with-skill and without-skill performed nearly identically across all 3 evals.

Eval 2 (existing transformer): Both variants replaced the existing transformer wholesale instead of extending it. This is a skill gap — the skill should explicitly instruct to EXTEND existing transformers, not replace them.
Eval 1 (greenfield): Both variants produced correct, comprehensive implementations with all 7 assertions passing.
Eval 3 (full implementation): Both variants correctly described backend steps without attempting to modify vets-api files.
The skill does not currently provide a measurable advantage over baseline. The model already knows the prefill pattern well from codebase context alone.
Key improvement area: Add explicit guidance in the skill about preserving existing prefillTransformer logic when one already exists.
recommended_skill_changes: Add a step in Phase 1 Step 3 (Create Prefill Transformer) that says: 'If a prefillTransformer already exists, EXTEND it rather than replacing it. Add the required fields (ssn, vaFileNumber, fullName) to the existing return structure while preserving all existing transformations.'
Add a clean-state check before creating the transformer: 'Does a prefillTransformer already exist? If yes, modify it to include the required fields.'
Consider adding more complex eval scenarios (e.g., forms with deeply nested data, forms with custom dataAdapter needs) where the skill's guidance would provide more differentiation.
